methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in the same sector plus one public peer (LNZA), with detailed guidance from three same-sector, adjacent-stage private companies: KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $380M. Peer-median analysis across the three comparable companies produced a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B. These methods triangulate to a low of $323M, median of $690M, and high of $1.1B.

The median implied valuation of $690M reflects meaningful uncertainty around technology risk, commercialization timeline, and the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($700M to $3.0B). The peer-median method carries particular weight given direct sector alignment, though it is constrained by a small sample. Stage-progression provides a conservative floor. Key uncertainties include path to revenue, competitive positioning relative to the peer set, and capital efficiency post-Series B.
